hero.pill: 20+ years of engineering — now in Germany
hero.title: Sees what your inspectors miss.
hero.sub: TFT deploys defect detection, safety monitoring, and production transparency on the cameras and systems you already have — backed by over 20 years of global engineering experience, now with a local team in Berlin.

demo.caption: ↳ Illustrative representation. Runs on your existing camera and network infrastructure — no new hardware rollout required.

problem.stat: One in three German manufacturing companies struggles to fill technical positions. The shortage is already affecting production, quality, and efficiency.
problem.title: The skilled-labor shortage has already reached the shop floor.
problem.lead: Quality issues. Safety risks. Delays. Not because manufacturers lack technology — but because experienced specialists are increasingly hard to replace.

card1.title: Experienced eyes are running short
card1.text: Inspection and monitoring roles are among the hardest positions to fill.

card2.title: Know-how leaves with every retirement
card2.text: With every retirement, years of hands-on knowledge disappear from the operation.

card3.title: Every investment has to pay off
card3.text: Manufacturers need measurable results — not years-long transformation projects.

comparison.title: Computer Vision: TFT vs. the alternatives
comparison.lead: Here's how TFT compares against the options a plant manager typically evaluates before committing to a computer vision pilot.

r3.label: Local point of contact in Berlin

comparison.note: Assessment as of June 2026. Individual results may vary — a given vendor's actual rating may differ on a case-by-case basis.

ctaStrip.text: Ready for a first look at your line? In a short conversation, we'll show you what a demo using your own camera or image footage could look like.

contact.title: 20 minutes. No pitch. Just an honest conversation.
contact.lead: Tell us where your bottleneck is — we'll tell you honestly whether and how we can help.
contact.leadEn: 20 minutes. No pitch. Just an honest conversation. Tell us where the bottleneck is — we will tell you honestly whether and how we can help.

solution.title: One Facility. Fully Visible.
solution.lead: See how computer vision works across your facility — from entry gates to production lines — using the cameras and systems you already have.

zones.productionSub: Industry-specific vision use cases — tap to explore

erp.main: ERP / MES / WMS / TMS integration layer — all vision data flows here
erp.sub: SAP · Odoo | Real-time alerts | Automated reporting | OEE / OTIF dashboards | Audit / traceability | AI agents
erp.compliance: IATF 16949 · EU GMP · ISO 22000 · ISO 9001 · 21 CFR Part 11 · HACCP · Blue Yonder · Manhattan

solution.hint: Tap any zone on the map to explore its computer vision use cases.

0.label: ANPR — plate read
0.desc: Auto-reads plates of incoming/outgoing trucks. Logs entry/exit times and verifies against expected deliveries.

1.label: Boom-barrier automation
1.desc: Opens/closes only for authorised, matched vehicles. No manual guard action needed.

2.label: Vehicle type classification
2.desc: Distinguishes car-carriers, reefer trucks, trailers, and vans. Routes to the correct dock or lane automatically.

3.label: Tailgating detection
3.desc: Flags an unauthorised vehicle following an authorised one through the gate.

4.label: JIT / time-slot check
4.desc: Verifies supplier trucks arrive within their booked delivery window. Flags early or late arrivals.

5.label: Inbound damage glance
5.desc: Quick visual scan of incoming part loads or packages for visible damage at the gate.

6.label: Finished-goods dispatch count
6.desc: Counts finished vehicles or pallets leaving. Reconciles against the dispatch manifest.

7.label: Cold-chain vehicle check
7.desc: Confirms reefer/cold-chain trucks are running and seals are intact before entry. (Food/Pharma)

0.label: Trailer / container ID (OCR)
0.desc: Reads ISO/BIC container and trailer numbers automatically at the gate.

1.label: Dock-appointment slot check
1.desc: Verifies carriers arrive within their booked time window before being waved in.

2.label: Driver / visitor verification
2.desc: Confirms driver identity against the booking and logs visitors automatically.

3.label: Seal-intact check at gate
3.desc: Confirms cargo seals are unbroken on arrival, before the trailer proceeds to dock.

worker.title: Worker entry / locker area
worker.sub: PPE, gowning, headcount — tuned per industry

0.label: Hard hat / helmet
0.desc: Confirms head protection is worn before entry to plant areas.

1.label: Hi-vis vest
1.desc: Confirms high-visibility jacket in plant, warehouse, and yard areas.

2.label: Safety shoes
2.desc: Confirms correct steel-cap or chemical-resistant footwear.

3.label: Gloves
3.desc: Checks hand protection in machining, chemical-handling and assembly zones.

4.label: Ear protection
4.desc: Checks earmuffs or plugs before entry to press, stamping, or high-noise zones.

5.label: Loose-clothing flag
5.desc: Flags loose sleeves or clothing near rotating machinery — a caught-in-machine risk.

6.label: Headcount / attendance
6.desc: Counts and logs workers entering via face or turnstile. Cross-references against shift roster.

7.label: Tailgating
7.desc: Enforces one-badge, one-person rule at every turnstile entry point.

0.label: Cleanroom gowning check
0.desc: Confirms coverall, hood, goggles, mask, gloves, and overshoes are all correctly worn.

1.label: Gowning sequence
1.desc: Verifies gowning is done in the correct GMP-mandated order before entering cleanroom.

2.label: No exposed skin / hair
2.desc: Flags any exposed skin or hair that could contaminate a clean or sterile area.

3.label: Double-gloving (sterile)
3.desc: Confirms second gloves are worn in Grade A/B sterile zones.

4.label: Air-shower compliance
4.desc: Confirms workers complete the full air-shower cycle before entering the cleanroom.

5.label: Zone-specific gowning
5.desc: Confirms gowning matches the grade of the cleanroom being entered (A/B/C/D).

6.label: De-gowning on exit
6.desc: Confirms correct de-gowning procedure is followed when leaving controlled areas.

7.label: Hand sanitization
7.desc: Confirms hand hygiene is performed at designated stations before entry.

0.label: Hairnet detection
0.desc: Confirms hairnet is fully covering hair before entry to production areas.

1.label: Beard net
1.desc: Confirms beard net where required by hygiene policy.

2.label: Food-grade gloves
2.desc: Confirms correct food-safe gloves including metal-detectable variants where required.

3.label: No jewellery / watches
3.desc: Flags rings, watches, earrings, and other items that could contaminate food.

4.label: Colour-coded uniform
4.desc: Confirms uniform colour matches the zone (raw vs cooked, allergen vs allergen-free).

5.label: Hand-wash compliance
5.desc: Confirms hand-washing and sanitization is completed before entry to production.

6.label: No exposed bandages
6.desc: Flags uncovered wounds or bandages — a contamination and metal-detection risk.

7.label: Apron / coat check
7.desc: Confirms apron or protective coat is worn and is clean before entry.

0.label: Hi-vis vest
0.desc: Confirms high-visibility vest is worn in warehouse and yard areas.

1.label: MHE-operator badge check
1.desc: Confirms forklift and MHE operators hold a valid, current certification badge.

2.label: Cold-store PPE
2.desc: Confirms thermal gear is worn before entry to cold-chain or freezer zones.

3.label: Safety shoes
3.desc: Confirms correct steel-cap footwear before entry to dock and yard areas.

4.label: Hard hat
4.desc: Confirms head protection near racking and MHE operating zones.

5.label: Gloves
5.desc: Checks hand protection is worn where required during handling tasks.

6.label: Headcount / attendance
6.desc: Counts and logs workers entering via face or turnstile recognition.

7.label: Tailgating
7.desc: Enforces one-badge, one-person rule at every turnstile entry point.

0.label: Crowd density / occupancy
0.desc: Monitors overcrowding at shift peaks. Alerts facility management to manage flow.

1.label: Queue length tracking
1.desc: Tracks canteen queue build-up and alerts when queues exceed acceptable thresholds.

2.label: Spill / slip detection
2.desc: Flags wet floors and spills before they become slip hazards. Auto-alerts cleaning team.

3.label: Hand hygiene at re-entry
3.desc: Confirms sanitization is completed before workers return to production areas.

4.label: De-gown before canteen (Pharma/Food)
4.desc: Confirms cleanroom or food-zone wear is removed before eating. Flags violations.

5.label: Re-gown after canteen
5.desc: Confirms correct re-gowning before production re-entry — a GMP and food-safety requirement.

6.label: No production garments in dining
6.desc: Flags production or cleanroom wear worn into the eating area — cross-contamination risk.

7.label: Pest detection (Food)
7.desc: Watches for pests in the dining zone — a critical audit point for food-safety certification.

0.label: Perimeter intrusion detection
0.desc: Detects fence climbing or boundary crossing. Triggers immediate alert to guards.

1.label: Camera-tamper detection
1.desc: Alerts if a camera is blocked, angled away, or physically moved.

2.label: Fire / smoke detection
2.desc: AI-enhanced early fire and smoke detection — especially critical in paint shops and solvent stores.

3.label: Restricted area monitoring
3.desc: Flags entry into unauthorised zones — R&D, formulation, vaults, sterile corridors.

4.label: Asset / scrap monitoring
4.desc: Watches for removal of materials, finished goods, or scrap outside authorised processes.

5.label: Finished-vehicle yard (Auto)
5.desc: Monitors high-value vehicle storage areas for damage or unauthorised access.

6.label: Cargo monitoring (Logistics)
6.desc: Watches high-value cargo and trailer areas for unauthorised handling or removal.

7.label: Unauthorized trailer movement
7.desc: Flags trailers moved from the yard or dock without dispatch authorisation.

0.label: Label OCR
0.desc: Auto-reads supplier labels, invoices, batch numbers, and expiry dates at receipt.

1.label: Barcode / QR read
1.desc: Scans part identifiers, serialization codes, and kanban cards at belt or pallet speed.

2.label: Carton / pallet count
2.desc: Counts cartons and pallets received or shipped. Reconciles against purchase or dispatch orders.

3.label: Inbound damage detection
3.desc: Inspects incoming packages and parts for visible damage. Photo evidence auto-generated for claims.

4.label: Dispatch vs order verify
4.desc: Confirms shipped items and quantities match the order before truck departure.

5.label: Tamper-evident seal check
5.desc: Confirms seals are intact on inbound and outbound goods. Flags breaches. (Pharma/Food)

6.label: Batch / expiry OCR
6.desc: Reads batch and expiry data on raw materials and finished goods at receipt and dispatch.

7.label: Dock-door management
7.desc: Monitors loading and unloading activity per dock. Logs timestamps and links to ERP.

0.label: ASN reconciliation
0.desc: Matches received goods against advance ship notices automatically.

1.label: Parcel dimensioning (DIM)
1.desc: Measures parcel dimensions on arrival for accurate billing and slotting.

2.label: Hazmat / DG placard read
2.desc: Detects and reads dangerous-goods placards on inbound and outbound loads.

3.label: Cold-chain temperature check
3.desc: Verifies temperature on temperature-sensitive receipts at the dock.

warehouse.title: Warehouse / storage
warehouse.sub: Inventory accuracy, pick verification, safety, compliance

0.label: Inventory counting
0.desc: Overhead or drone-mounted cameras count pallets and stock on racks — continuous or scheduled.

1.label: Empty-slot detection
1.desc: Finds open rack locations and feeds the WMS for smarter putaway routing.

2.label: Pick accuracy verification
2.desc: Confirms the correct item and quantity was picked before it leaves the station.

3.label: Misplaced-item alert
3.desc: Flags stock stored in the wrong location — prevents order errors and delays.

4.label: Forklift / pedestrian safety
4.desc: Detects when pedestrians enter forklift operating zones. Alerts driver and supervisor.

5.label: FEFO / FIFO enforcement
5.desc: Confirms oldest or first-expiry stock is picked first — critical in pharma and food.

6.label: Temperature-zone compliance
6.desc: Confirms goods are stored in the correct temperature zone. (Pharma / Food / Cold chain)

7.label: Batch traceability
7.desc: Links stored stock visually to batch records — supports fast recall readiness.

0.label: Yard / trailer location tracking
0.desc: Tracks where each trailer is parked across the yard in real time.

1.label: Trailer occupancy (loaded/empty)
1.desc: Detects whether each parked trailer is loaded or empty without manual checks.

2.label: Dock-door availability
2.desc: Shows which dock doors are free or occupied to speed up trailer assignment.

3.label: Vehicle / trailer damage inspection
3.desc: Inspects vehicles in and out of the yard for visible damage, with photo evidence.

4.label: Load-securement check
4.desc: Confirms loads are properly secured before a trailer departs the yard.

5.label: Seal verification at dispatch
5.desc: Confirms seals are correctly applied at departure and logs the seal number.

6.label: Driver fatigue / distraction
6.desc: In-cab monitoring for fleet safety on outbound and inbound routes (where deployed).

7.label: Drone-based stock audit
7.desc: Aerial cycle counts of high racking and yard stock for fast, accurate audits.

cleaning.title: Cleaning / maintenance
cleaning.sub: Verify activity, compliance, and audit evidence

0.label: Cleaning-activity detection
0.desc: Confirms cleaning crews actually cleaned zones — detects mop, bucket, and movement patterns.

1.label: Schedule compliance
1.desc: Verifies cleaning is done within the required time window. Flags missed or late cleans.

2.label: 5S / housekeeping compliance
2.desc: Monitors workplace organisation — flags items left in wrong places or aisles blocked.

3.label: Spill cleanup verification
3.desc: Confirms spills are cleaned up after alert. Closes the loop on the incident log.

4.label: CIP verification (Food)
4.desc: Confirms clean-in-place cycles ran on equipment. Visual evidence for HACCP and ISO 22000.

5.label: Line-clearance (Pharma)
5.desc: Confirms the area is cleared between batches — a GMP-critical step before next production run.

6.label: Disinfection verification
6.desc: Confirms disinfection was performed in the correct sequence and areas.

7.label: Audit-log trail
7.desc: Timestamped visual record of every cleaning event — ready for ISO, GMP, HACCP, or BG audits.

production.title: Production floor
production.sub: Select your industry — use cases differ significantly

0.label: Paint defects
0.desc: Detects orange peel, runs, drips, dust nibs, and scratches on painted bodies.

1.label: Weld quality
1.desc: Detects spatter, missing welds, and bad weld beads at every station.

2.label: Body-panel dents
2.desc: Surface dent and scratch detection on sheet metal and body panels.

3.label: Panel gap & flush
3.desc: Measures gaps and alignment between panels against spec tolerances.

4.label: Fastener / bolt presence
4.desc: Checks all bolts and screws are present and correctly torqued at each station.

5.label: Harness routing
5.desc: Verifies wiring harness is routed correctly and clipped in the right positions.

6.label: Variant / VIN verification
6.desc: Confirms correct trim and engine variant per build sheet. Matches VIN to order spec.

7.label: Poka-yoke error-proofing
7.desc: Catches assembly errors at the station before the car moves to the next step.

8.label: Robotic bin-picking guidance
8.desc: Vision guides robots picking randomly placed parts from bins — no fixed orientation required.

9.label: Dimensional measurement
9.desc: In-line measurement of part dimensions against CAD. Flags out-of-tolerance items before assembly.

10.label: Stamping-die monitoring
10.desc: Watches press-die condition for wear, crack, and misalignment — predicts failure before scrap.

11.label: Wheel / tyre fitment check
11.desc: Verifies correct wheel and tyre assembly before the vehicle leaves the line.

0.label: Tablet / capsule defects
0.desc: Detects chips, cracks, colour variation, spots, twins, and double-caps at full line speed.

1.label: Vial defects
1.desc: Detects cracks, particulates in liquid, fill-level variation, and cap/crimp faults.

2.label: Blister-pack defects
2.desc: Detects empty pockets, broken, or misformed tablets in blister packaging.

3.label: Particulate inspection
3.desc: Checks injectables and liquid products for visible particulates.

4.label: Label correctness
4.desc: Confirms batch, expiry, and product name on labels match the batch record.

5.label: Braille verification
5.desc: Confirms required Braille text is present and correctly embossed (EU requirement).

6.label: Serialization code check
6.desc: Confirms serial codes are present, scannable, and match the track-and-trace record.

7.label: Correct leaflet insertion
7.desc: Confirms the right patient information leaflet is inserted into every carton.

8.label: Line-clearance verification
8.desc: Confirms no leftover material from the previous batch remains on the line — a GMP-critical check.

9.label: Fill-finish / isolator monitoring
9.desc: Watches activity in sterile fill-finish areas and RABS/isolator environments.

10.label: Visual batch record
10.desc: Captures a visual record of every production step for electronic batch record and 21 CFR Part 11 audit.

11.label: Tablet-press monitoring
11.desc: Watches tablet-press condition for die wear, sticking, and compression variation.

0.label: Foreign-object detection
0.desc: Detects metal, plastic, glass, insects, and stones — often combined with X-ray inspection.

1.label: Appearance check
1.desc: Checks colour, shape, size, burn marks, and mould on food products at line speed.

2.label: Contamination detection
2.desc: Flags visible contamination on the product or on the line surface.

3.label: Under / over-cook detection
3.desc: Flags improperly cooked product using colour and thermal analysis.

4.label: Date-code / batch legibility
4.desc: Confirms codes are present, readable, and correct before packs leave the line.

5.label: Fill level check
5.desc: Checks fill level in packs and containers — flags under and overfill.

6.label: Seal integrity
6.desc: Detects heat-seal and closure defects that could compromise shelf life.

7.label: Allergen changeover clearance
7.desc: Confirms the line is fully cleared between allergen and allergen-free product runs.

8.label: Quality grading / sorting
8.desc: Sorts produce by ripeness, size, colour, or grade — replaces or augments manual graders.

9.label: Pest detection on line
9.desc: Flags pests in the production area — a critical control point for food-safety certification.

10.label: On-floor gowning compliance
10.desc: Confirms hairnet and gloves are worn correctly by all workers on the production line.

11.label: Portion / weight estimation
11.desc: Estimates portion size or weight using vision — flags deviations before packing.

0.label: Surface defects
0.desc: Detects scratches, cracks, dents, and discolouration on finished parts.

1.label: Moulding defects
1.desc: Detects flash, short shots, and sink marks in plastic and cast components.

2.label: Print / label defects
2.desc: Detects misprints, smudges, and misalignment on packaging and product labels.

3.label: Dimensional check
3.desc: Flags out-of-tolerance dimensions in-line — feeds rejection gate automatically.

4.label: Component presence check
4.desc: Confirms all parts are present and correctly oriented before the unit moves forward.

5.label: Screw / fastener count
5.desc: Checks fastener count and seating at each assembly station.

6.label: In-line product count
6.desc: Counts products on the conveyor. Reconciles against production targets and ERP order.

7.label: Fill-level & weight verify
7.desc: Checks product weight and fill levels in containers against specification.

8.label: Motor / bearing thermal
8.desc: Thermal camera detects overheating motors and bearings before failure.

9.label: Conveyor belt health
9.desc: Vision spots belt wear, roller damage, and tracking issues before they cause downtime.

10.label: Vibration-by-vision
10.desc: Detects abnormal machine vibration patterns using high-speed cameras — no sensor required.

11.label: Coolant / oil leak
11.desc: Detects leaks on the floor or machine surfaces before they cause secondary damage.

0.label: Sort-label / barcode read
0.desc: Reads labels on the conveyor at speed to drive sort-lane routing decisions.

1.label: Destination / address OCR
1.desc: Reads shipping addresses to support automated sort decisions.

2.label: Misrouted-parcel detection
2.desc: Flags parcels sent to the wrong sort lane before they leave the line.

3.label: Conveyor jam / blockage detection
3.desc: Detects jams and blockages on the sort line and alerts maintenance.

4.label: Damaged / crushed parcel detection
4.desc: Flags damaged packages on the line for rework or claims.

5.label: Open / leaking package detection
5.desc: Flags burst, open, or leaking parcels before they continue downstream.

6.label: Non-conveyable / oversized detection
6.desc: Identifies irregular or oversized items that need manual handling.

7.label: Wrong-item / wrong-label detection
7.desc: Flags a mismatch between the item and the label attached to it.

8.label: Truck-load verification
8.desc: Confirms the count and items loaded match the manifest per truck.

9.label: Trailer fill / cube utilization
9.desc: Measures how fully each trailer is loaded to optimise cube usage.

10.label: Mis-load detection
10.desc: Flags a parcel loaded onto the wrong route or truck before departure.

11.label: Dock-door activity monitoring
11.desc: Tracks loading and unloading activity at every dock door.

12.label: Conveyor / sorter health
12.desc: Monitors automation equipment condition to predict failures before downtime.

13.label: AGV / AMR navigation & obstacle detection
13.desc: Guides autonomous vehicles around the facility and avoids collisions.

14.label: Robotic pick / place guidance
14.desc: Vision-guides robotic handling for picking and placing parcels or items.

15.label: Forklift / MHE safety
15.desc: Detects pedestrian proximity, speeding, and near-misses around MHE.

presence.heading: We are on the ground — not just online.
presence.cardBody:
Think Future Technologies GmbH
Klara-Franke-Str. 16
10557 Berlin, Germany
info@tftus.com

footer.tagline: Engineering acceleration for German companies — specialized, certified, locally present.

cookies.text: We use cookies to operate and improve our website. See our Cookie Policy and Privacy Policy for more information.
